data.title: AI TRENDS | ZetaChain Integrates Google's Gemma 4 AI Model into Anuma AI Layer
data.description: ZetaChain has integrated Google's newly launched Gemma 4 AI model into its Anuma AI layer, just a day after the model's official release. According to NS3.AI, ZetaChain announced that the Gemma 4 model is now accessible on Anuma without requiring separate installation. The company highlighted that Gemma 4 outperforms models that are 20 times its size.

data.title: Uniswap Introduces Automatic Routing Feature Across Its Platforms
data.description: Uniswap has launched an automatic routing feature on its web application, wallet, and API. According to Foresight News, this new functionality eliminates the need for a whitelist process, allowing users to build their own Hooks and deploy liquidity pools to start distribution from day one. As a security measure, each transaction is simulated, and if the chosen path might lead to a failure, the system automatically selects an alternative route for the transaction. Hooks that do not meet the automatic routing standards will still undergo manual review by the team.

data.title: Prediction Market Trading Volume Rises in March 2026
data.description: Prediction market trading volume reached $25.7 billion in March 2026, marking a 10.6% increase from February's $23.2 billion, according to NS3.AI. Kalshi led the market with a trading volume of $13.1 billion, while Polymarket followed closely with $10.6 billion.

data.title: Hermes Agent v0.7.0 Introduces Scalable Plugin System
data.description:
Nous Research has announced the release of Hermes Agent v0.7.0. According to Foresight News, the latest version features a scalable plugin system for memory, allowing users to switch between different backends or build their own. The built-in memory is immediately available and supports integration with six third-party providers, offering users the option to select 'hermes memory setup.'

Additional highlights include a credential pool for multiple providers, enabling automatic rotation and failover of multiple API keys per provider. The Camofox browser offers a stealth browsing backend for persistent sessions, VNC debugging, and automatic installation. Inline difference previews allow users to view changes in file writing and patch display tools before the agent proceeds.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Meta Suspends Collaboration with Mercor Following Data Breach
data.description: Meta has suspended its collaboration with AI startup Mercor following a recent data breach, according to a report by Business Insider. A source familiar with the matter confirmed the suspension and stated that Meta is conducting an investigation into the incident. Mercor, which was valued at $10 billion during a funding round last October, collaborates with major tech companies like Meta to train AI models with the assistance of thousands of contractors and experts. According to Jin10, Wired first reported last Friday that Meta had halted all cooperation with Mercor. While Meta declined to comment, Mercor confirmed that it had indeed experienced a security breach.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Anthropic's Claude Mythos Release Odds Decline
data.description: The probability of Anthropic releasing Claude Mythos before April 30 has decreased significantly, according to recent data. According to NS3.AI, Polymarket odds have dropped to 28%, marking a 26 percentage point decline over the past week. The April contract saw approximately $42,000 in trading volume. Meanwhile, a separate contract for June is priced at 53%, with a trading volume of about $11,600.

data.title: The notional trading volume of the market is projected to reach $25.7 billion in March 2026, with Kalshi topping the list with a trading volume of $13.1 billion.
data.description: Data analyst dash stated in an article published on the X platform that the nominal trading volume of the forecast market will reach $25.7 billion in March 2026, a 10.6% increase compared to $23.2 billion in February 2026. Specific data for each platform is as follows: Kalshi: $13.1 billion (up 25.2% QoQ); Polymarket: $10.6 billion (up 33.1% QoQ); Crypto.com: $629.7 million (up 58.5% QoQ); Opinion: $496.2 million (down 84.0% QoQ); Limitless: $464.5 million (up 25.2% QoQ); Predict: $329.7 million (down 58.0% QoQ); Myriad: $60.4 million (down 51.7% QoQ); IBKR: $41.2 million (down 35.0% QoQ); Gemini: $19 million (up 1932.1% QoQ); Overtime: $17.9 million (up 2.4% QoQ).

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Chinese Gold Jewelry Prices Remain Stable
data.description: On April 4, Jin10 reported that the prices of gold jewelry in China have shown little change compared to the previous day. According to Jin10, several brands have maintained their gold jewelry prices within the range of 1,430 yuan to 1,450 yuan per gram.

data.title: Polymarket's probability that "Claude Mythos's model will be released before April 30th" has dropped to 28%.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer, the probability of a Claude Mythos model being released in April on Polymarket has plummeted to 28%, a drop of 26% in the past 7 days, with approximately $42,000 in bets. The probability of a Claude Mythos model being released in June is currently reported at 53%, with approximately $11,600 in bets. The event contract rules are as follows: if Anthropic releases "Claude Mythos" or confirms that it is the same model mentioned in the leak, and that model is made available to the public on a specified date (Eastern Time), the market will decide "yes". Otherwise, the market will ultimately decide "no". Eligible models must be named "Claude Mythos" (e.g., Claude Mythos 1, Claude Mythos 5, Claude Mythos X, etc.), or confirmed to be the same model mentioned by Anthropic in the leak, or confirmed by consensus through credible reporting. Products labeled as Claude Haiku, Sonnet, and Opus 4.7/5.0 or similar will not be included in this market's evaluation unless confirmed to be the same model mentioned in the leaked information. For this market to be officially recognized, eligible models (as defined above) must be online and publicly available, including through open testing or open rolling registration. Closed testing or any form of private access does not qualify. Previously, Anthropic announced a ban on free subscriptions, and OpenClaw requires pay-as-you-go billing or API usage for Claude. Odaily Seer continues to monitor the prediction market, seeing changes before pricing.

data.title: Anthropic announced a ban on users subscribing to "free" services; OpenClaw requires users to pay-as-you-go or use the API to use Claude.
data.description: Anthropic, the parent company of Claude, announced that starting at 3:00 AM Beijing time on April 5th, it will stop allowing users to call Claude models in third-party tools via subscription. AI Agent tools, including OpenClaw, will switch to pay-as-you-go billing. Related calls will require the use of the API or an additional paid subscription. Claude Code CEO Boris Cherny stated, “We have been working hard to meet the growing demand for Claude, and subscriptions are not designed for the usage patterns of these third-party tools. Capacity is a carefully managed resource, prioritizing customer use of our products and APIs.” This move is intended to “consciously manage growth and continuously achieve long-term sustainable service.”

data.title: Altcoin Market Shows Mixed Performance Amidst Sideways Crypto Trends
data.description: The altcoin market has exhibited varied performance this week, with the overall cryptocurrency market experiencing a sideways trend. According to Odaily, Delphi Digital shared insights on Platform X, highlighting the top five gainers in the altcoin sector. Leading the list is VVV with a 21.87% increase, followed by Render at 18.06%, Iota at 15%, MON at 13.33%, and JUP at 13.2%.

data.title: Telegram Usage in Russia and Iran Surges Despite Government Restrictions
data.description:
On April 4, Telegram founder Pavel Durov announced on his personal channel that despite long-standing restrictions in Russia, approximately 65 million Russian users continue to access the app daily using VPNs and other methods. According to BlockBeats, over 50 million of these users remain active in daily messaging.

Meanwhile, the Russian government is intensifying efforts to block VPNs, facing technical challenges in implementing these measures. Recent reports indicate that these network restrictions have occasionally disrupted payment systems, leading some regions to temporarily revert to cash transactions.

A similar situation is unfolding in Iran, where the ban on Telegram was intended to promote local, government-regulated communication apps. Instead, it has led to a significant increase in VPN usage, creating widespread "digital bypass" phenomena.

Analysts suggest that in environments with strict regulations, the demand for communication freedom and privacy persists, driving technological countermeasures to evolve. As more developers engage in building VPNs and proxy tools, the struggle between information access and regulation is intensifying.

data.title: Delphi Digital: The altcoin market performance is mixed, with VVV topping the weekly gainers list with a 21.87% increase.
data.description: Delphi Digital published an article on the X platform stating that the altcoin market performance remained mixed this week, with the crypto market trending sideways. The top five gainers were: • VVV (+21.87%) • Render (+18.06%) • Iota (+15%) • MON (+13.33%) • JUP (+13.2%)

data.title: Canadian Central Bank Analyzes Risks in DeFi Lending
data.description: The Canadian central bank has released a report titled 'DeFi Lending: Returns, Leverage, and Liquidation Risks.' According to Foresight News, the report examines decentralized lending on Aave V3, utilizing transaction-level data to analyze its revenue model, borrower behavior, and liquidation dynamics. The findings indicate that protocol revenues are concentrated in a few tokens, and many users engage in recursive leverage operations despite over-collateralization requirements. Liquidations occur in concentrated waves but have limited impact on the broader market. Overall, while DeFi lending is operationally feasible with proper governance, it faces constraints related to capital efficiency, liquidation risks, and vulnerabilities within the crypto ecosystem.

data.title: Coinglass Q1 Crypto Market Report: Derivatives Remain Dominant, Binance Leads Across Multiple Metrics
data.description: According to Coinglass's 2026 Q1 Cryptocurrency Market Share Research Report, the overall cryptocurrency market maintained relatively high activity levels, though trading volumes gradually declined from their January highs. Total market spot volume in Q1 was approximately $1.94 trillion, derivatives volume approximately $18.63 trillion, totaling roughly $20.57 trillion, with a derivatives-to-spot ratio of about 9.6x. Structurally, market activity remained primarily concentrated in the derivatives market, while spot trading contracted by a relatively larger margin.Binance retained industry leadership on core metrics: derivatives volume of approximately $4.90 trillion represented a share of about 34.9% among the top 10 exchanges, average daily open interest of approximately $23.9 billion corresponded to a 29.9% share, and user asset reserves of approximately $152.9 billion accounted for about 73.5% among major centralized exchanges.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Solana Foundation Introduces Solana Agent Skills for AI Integration
data.description:
The Solana Foundation has announced the launch of Solana Agent Skills, which offers pre-built skills that can be directly integrated into AI tools. According to Foresight News, these skills can be installed with a single line of code, enabling the creation of agents capable of interacting with Solana.

The official skills include features such as common error handling, security checklists, confidential transfers, and version compatibility. Additionally, over 60 community-developed skills are available, covering areas like DeFi, payments, infrastructure, and tools. These skills are sourced from platforms such as JupiterExchange, Raydium, Helius, dflow, and Metaplex.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Anthropic Adjusts Claude Subscription Policy
data.description: Anthropic has announced changes to its Claude subscription policy. According to Foresight News, starting April 5 at 3:00 UTC+8, the subscription will no longer include access to third-party tools such as OpenClaw. Users can still utilize these tools by purchasing additional usage packages, which are now available at a discount, or by using a Claude API key to log in with their Claude account.

data.title: Bitcoin ETFs Could Surpass Gold ETFs in Asset Management, Analyst Predicts
data.description: ETF analyst James Seyffart has suggested that Bitcoin ETFs might soon exceed gold ETFs in terms of total assets under management. According to NS3.AI, U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs attracted $1.32 billion in inflows during March, while U.S. gold ETFs experienced outflows totaling $2.92 billion. Seyffart noted that Bitcoin offers more diverse portfolio roles compared to gold, potentially driving its increasing popularity among investors.

data.title: Solana launches Agent Skills, enabling AI agents to interact with on-chain data.
data.description: The Solana Foundation announced the launch of Solana Agent Skills, providing pre-built functional modules that can be integrated into AI tools, allowing users to interact with the Solana blockchain through simple invocation. Official skills cover error handling, security checks, confidential transfers, and version compatibility. In addition, over 60 community skills have been launched simultaneously, covering areas such as DeFi, payments, and infrastructure, with participants including Jupiter Exchange, Raydium, Helius, dflow, and Metaplex.

data.title: Market Pricing Indicates Decreased Bets on Fed Rate Cuts in 2026
data.description: According to Jin10, market pricing data as of April 3 suggests a decline in bets on the Federal Reserve cutting interest rates in 2026. This shift in market sentiment reflects changing expectations regarding the Fed's monetary policy trajectory. Investors are closely monitoring economic indicators and Fed communications for further insights into potential rate adjustments.
